decisions_or_constraints: Implementation remains blocked until /sp.analyze validates the task package; tasks.md correctly recommends /sp.analyze as the next command.

The dependency graph is phase-serial with intra-phase parallel opportunities, but several edges need to be made explicit in task-index.json to keep downstream analysis from relying on prose-only ordering.

Shared write surfaces require serial checkpoints: scripts/quality-gate/types.ts, scripts/quality-gate/modes.ts, scripts/quality-gate/runner.ts, scripts/pr/impact-report.ts, scripts/pr/change-policy.ts, and scripts/quality-gate/__tests__/routing.test.ts.
Fast-lane success must remain a development signal only; no task may present fast mode as PR-ready or merge-ready.
Coverage behavior is an explicit planning decision: changed-line coverage for PR mode and full coverage for baseline/release modes, with ratchets preserved.

dependency_edges.reason: types work follows quality-gate pattern inspection
fast mode definition needs QualityGateMode type support
package script should target an existing fast mode
impact-report changes require PR impact/change-policy pattern inspection
test selection must align with the fast mode lane shape
fast-lane-tests lane consumes impact-report selection output
core-smoke lane is added to the fast mode composition
runtime measurement needs the smoke lane in place
PR layering needs fast-lane-tests defined
PR layering needs core-smoke defined
runner filtering follows finalized PR lane ordering
RiskLevel extends shared types after mode type setup
change-policy risk rules need RiskLevel type
risk rule changes require change-policy pattern inspection
impact report escalatedChecks depend on risk classification
impact-report escalation extends the same output surface as fast-lane tests
modes escalation rules consume impact-report escalatedChecks
escalated lane gating depends on PR lane structure
report fields extend shared quality-gate types
runner report semantics require typed fields
readyForMerge semantics depend on finalized runner mode execution
reporter banners consume runner report fields
coverageMode extends shared lane configuration types
mode definitions need typed coverageMode field
coverage mode assignment depends on finalized PR mode layering
runner passes --changed based on mode coverage configuration
coverage runner changes share runner.ts with output semantics
fast mode tests expect the quality:fast script to exist
routing tests validate fast-lane-tests lane composition
routing tests validate core-smoke lane composition
changed-area tests extend the same routing test file
changed-area tests depend on impact-report selection logic
risk tests extend the same routing test file after selection coverage
risk tests depend on escalatedChecks output
output tests extend the same routing test file
output tests depend on reporter labels
coverage tests extend the same routing test file
coverage tests depend on runner coverageMode behavior
final validation follows all regression tests
quickstart timing/results must reflect actual validation output

join_points.name: Guardrails Confirmed
join_points.validation: Manual confirmation that lane definition, impact filtering, and risk classification patterns are understood before source edits.

join_points.name: Fast Mode Defined
join_points.validation: bun run quality:fast --dry-run completes and fast mode excludes heavy lanes.

join_points.name: Test Selection Working
join_points.validation: Changed-area fast lane executes only the selected area tests for a representative changed path.

join_points.name: Core Smoke Validated
join_points.validation: Core smoke always runs and stays under the 20 second budget.

join_points.name: PR Mode Layered
join_points.validation: bun run verify preserves required lanes while ordering fast checks before heavier checks.

join_points.name: Risk Escalation Working
join_points.validation: Fixture changes for native, provider/runtime, and release-sensitive paths trigger their escalated lanes.

join_points.name: Output Semantics Clear
join_points.validation: Fast reports show development-signal/not-PR-ready semantics; PR reports show PR readiness semantics.

join_points.name: Coverage Strategy Working
join_points.validation: PR mode uses changed-line coverage and baseline/release modes retain full coverage.

join_points.name: All Regression Tests Pass
join_points.validation: bun test scripts/quality-gate/__tests__/routing.test.ts passes and covers SC-001 through SC-006.

join_points.name: Final Validation Complete
join_points.validation: Actual fast/verify timing and validation results are available before quickstart updates.

high_risk_review_gates.risk: Multiple branches extend scripts/quality-gate/types.ts; downstream modes, runner, and reporter depend on compatible shared types.
high_risk_review_gates.checkpoint: After all type extensions, verify runner and routing tests compile before modes/runner tasks continue.

high_risk_review_gates.risk: scripts/quality-gate/modes.ts is a shared registration surface for fast, PR, baseline, and release behavior.
high_risk_review_gates.checkpoint: Require read-only review of final lane composition to ensure heavy checks are conditional, not removed, and existing modes remain intact.

high_risk_review_gates.risk: scripts/quality-gate/runner.ts controls filtering, report fields, and coverage flags; mistakes can silently skip checks or mislabel readiness.
high_risk_review_gates.checkpoint: Review runner diff plus routing tests before declaring PR mode or coverage strategy valid.

high_risk_review_gates.risk: Misclassification can skip same-area tests or escalated checks for high-risk paths.
high_risk_review_gates.checkpoint: Require fixture-based validation for server, desktop, adapters, docs, native, provider/runtime, and release-sensitive paths.

high_risk_review_gates.risk: Sequential edits to one test file can mask missing scenarios unless each layer is independently validated.
high_risk_review_gates.checkpoint: Run the focused routing test after each incremental test task and final full focused suite before T027.

task_adjustments.issue: T008 is marked parallel_safe but writes scripts/quality-gate/modes.ts, the same shared surface as T004, T007, T010, T015, and T020.
task_adjustments.recommended_fix: Keep T008 dependent on T004 and ensure task-index.json records it cannot run in the same batch as T007/T010/T015/T020; treat its [P] marker as only eligible after the Fast Mode Defined join point and with no concurrent modes.ts writers.

task_adjustments.issue: T012, T016, and T019 all depend only on T003 and each writes scripts/quality-gate/types.ts, creating a potential parallel write conflict despite [P] markers.
task_adjustments.recommended_fix: Either serialize the type-extension tasks in task-index.json (for example T012 -> T016 -> T019) or merge them behind a single shared-types join task before downstream consumers T013, T017, and T020.

task_adjustments.issue: T005 is listed as parallel_safe but depends on T004, so it is not actually parallel with T004 despite the Parallel Execution Opportunities text saying it can run in parallel with T004 after T004 completes.
task_adjustments.recommended_fix: Revise the parallel opportunity note: T005 is an isolated follow-up after T004, not a same-batch parallel lane.

task_adjustments.issue: T006 is currently ordered before T008, but plan Capability 1 says fast lane equals changed-area tests plus core smoke. T007 wires fast-lane-tests before core-smoke is defined.
task_adjustments.recommended_fix: Add an explicit join that fast mode is provisional after T007 until T008 completes, or add T008 as a dependency of the fast-mode completeness checkpoint and any tests that assert complete fast lane composition.

task_adjustments.issue: T013 can begin after T012 and T002 while T006 may still be modifying impact-report assumptions; this is safe for write sets but not for final escalation semantics.
task_adjustments.recommended_fix: Keep T013 implementation parallel-safe by write set, but require the Risk Escalation Working join point to include T006/T014 evidence before T015 wires escalated lanes.

task_adjustments.issue: T022 through T026 serialize all regression tests in a single file, delaying test-first coverage until after implementation tasks.
task_adjustments.recommended_fix: If /sp.analyze enforces test-before-implementation strictly, split test tasks earlier by surface or add explicit rationale that this feature uses post-implementation regression tests due to route/mode APIs being stabilized first.

task_adjustments.issue: T028 uses agent doc-updater, which is not in the sp-tasks allowed agent list in the loaded workflow contract.
task_adjustments.recommended_fix: Change T028 agent to executor or writer-compatible allowed role if available in the execution environment; update task-index.json consistently.

task_adjustments.issue: task-index.json shows no dependency_cycles or write_set_conflicts, but it does not account for potential conflicts among parallel_safe tasks sharing types.ts.
task_adjustments.recommended_fix: Update task-index.json write_set_conflicts or parallel batch metadata to flag T012/T016/T019 as mutually exclusive unless serialized.

task_adjustments.issue: No task-packets directory entries were present during this lane's inspection.
task_adjustments.recommended_fix: Before implementation handoff, generate per-task packets or have /sp.analyze block DP packet readiness until task-packets/*.json exist and match tasks.md/task-index.json.

dp_readiness.DP1: Partial: tasks.md and task-index.json exist with 28 tasks and no recorded cycles, but per-task packet files were not present during inspection, so dispatch packet readiness should not be considered complete.
dp_readiness.DP2: Partial: dependency ordering is broadly acyclic and phase-serial, but analyze should require explicit serialization or conflict metadata for shared types.ts and modes.ts writers before approving parallel dispatch.
dp_readiness.DP3: Blocked for implementation: next command remains /sp.analyze, and implementation must not start until analyze validates graph fixes, packet completeness, guardrail preservation, and DP1/DP2 readiness.

validation_evidence: task-index.json reports total_tasks=28, write_set_conflicts=[], and dependency_cycles=[].
tasks.md declares Serial phases with intra-phase parallel batches and recommends /sp.analyze as the next command.
handoff-to-tasks.json maps SC-001 through SC-006 to task IDs and recommends /sp.analyze.
plan.md preserves LD-001 through LD-007 and resolves MP-012 as changed-line PR coverage with full baseline/release coverage.
context.md identifies the same primary affected surfaces used by tasks.md: modes.ts, runner.ts, impact-report.ts, change-policy.ts, types.ts, package.json, and tests.
No task-packets entries were found when inspecting .specify/features/003-layered-test-verify/task-packets, so packet readiness is partial from this lane's view.
concerns: No obvious cycles or impossible total ordering were found, but current parallel-safe markers are optimistic for shared type and mode registration surfaces.

The dependency graph in tasks.md visually omits some edges present in task-index.json, including T004 -> T008, T003 -> T012/T016/T019, and T018 -> T025; downstream tools should use task-index.json only after it is corrected.

Regression tests are scheduled after most implementation work; this may be acceptable for route-level integration tests, but analyze should verify the project testing contract does not require earlier test tasks.
T028's doc-updater role may be invalid under the loaded sp-tasks agent roster.
Per-task packet files appear absent, which weakens independent executability despite tasks.md containing rich task bodies.
recovery_hints: Run /sp.analyze before implementation and have it specifically validate shared-surface write conflicts, packet presence, and invalid agent roles.
If analyze blocks, first repair task-index.json and tasks.md parallel metadata; do not edit source code or implementation files.
Prefer adding serial join tasks or explicit mutual-exclusion metadata for shared registration surfaces instead of allowing concurrent writers.
Generate or refresh task-packets/*.json from the corrected task graph before dispatching implementation subagents.
